cognates.correspondence_path: Ar ل ↔ He ל; Ar ب ↔ He ב; Ar ن ↔ He ן — consistent *l-b-n root.
cognates.notes: Semantic development: Hebrew uses the root for 'white' (l-b-n), while in Arabic it refers specifically to 'milk' (which is white).

cognates.correspondence_path: Syc ܠ ↔ He ל; Syc ܒ ↔ He ב; Syc ܢ ↔ He ן — phonologically stable.
cognates.notes: Cognate maintains the 'white' semantic field, also associated with bricks/whitewashing.

caveats: v0 prototype: this is an LLM-generated analysis, not a lexicon lookup. Results may be incomplete or incorrect, especially for Syriac, Tigrinya, and rare forms. Verify against Wiktionary or a scholarly lexicon before citing.

The root l-b-n universally relates to whiteness across Semitic languages, but semantic divergence (e.g., 'milk' in Arabic, 'brick' in Hebrew) illustrates the difficulty of tracking secondary lexical derivations.
